g.text: Geometric Intuition: Rotating Hands on a Clock

div.p: Key Property: Each position has a unique 512-dimensional "fingerprint" The combination of sin/cos waves at different frequencies ensures:

ul.li: Relative position can be learned: PE(pos+k) = f(PE(pos), k)
Extrapolates to longer sequences than seen during training

div.p: Shape: [5000, 512] for max_seq_len=5000, d_model=512 Visualized as heatmap:

ul.li: Color: sin/cos value (-1 to +1) Pattern: Alternating bands of different frequencies Low dims: wide bands (slow waves) High dims: narrow bands (fast waves)

p.strong: Think of each dimension pair as clock hands:

ul.li: ... up to 256 "clocks" running at different speeds Result: A unique "timestamp" for each position that encodes both absolute and relative position.

div.p: Why 10000? Base frequency that gives good coverage:

ul.li: At i=255: wavelength = 2π × 10000 ≈ 62,832 positions This range spans ~4 orders of magnitude, covering both fine and coarse position granularity. The choice is empirical but works well across model sizes.

div.p: sin(pos/1) — cycles every 2π positions Very slow oscillation, encodes absolute position
sin(pos/100) — moderate wavelength Encodes medium-range positional relationships
sin(pos/10) — faster oscillation Encodes fine-grained position differences
sin(pos/0.0001) — very fast oscillation Almost unique value per position
